urn:contentItem:6JJ0-JKF3-RTBS-751X-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:42ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - UPDATES FROM PEERS ACROSS THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION SPOTLIGHTS MID-MARKET OPPORTUNITIES AT NEW YORK FORUM

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) hosted the 28th 'New York Finance Forum' at the Korea Society in New York, focusing on the burgeoning mid-market segment in North American infrastructure investments. The event took place on the 16th of April 2026, drawing over 40 participants from government, public investment institutions, and various financial sectors.During the forum, Northleaf Capital Partners presented key insights into the mid-market infrastructure investment landscape, highlighting its structural advantages. This segment typically includes deals involving companies valued at approximately USD 1 billion or less, though the threshold has recently expanded to include transactions up to USD 2 billion depending on market dynamics and asset manager criteria.Jared Waldron, Co-Head of Infrastructure at Northleaf, underscored that despite 86% of North American infrastructure investment transactions occurring within the mid-market, institutional funds remain heavily concentrated in larger funds. This disparity creates a less competitive environment, allowing investors to secure more favorable investment opportunities.Jessica Kennedy, Managing Director at Northleaf, further elaborated on the advantages of mid-market infrastructure investments, noting that they offer opportunities for differentiated results through active value creation and multiple exit strategies. Investors can thus anticipate attractive risk-adjusted returns from this segment.Yuryoung Kim, Head of KIC's New York office and organizer of the forum, expressed optimism that the discussions would assist domestic institutional investors in refining their overseas alternative investment strategies. KIC continues to operate in major global financial hubs, including New York, San Francisco, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, facilitating the exchange of investment information and fostering collaborative networks.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

value.Title: FH Capital to Acquire 75.1% Majority Stake in JinkoSolar's U.S. Manufacturing Operations, Establishing a Premier Domestic Solar and BESS Platform

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-BFN3-S86W-34JC-00000-00FH Capital to Acquire 75.1% Majority Stake in JinkoSolar's U.S. Manufacturing Operations, Establishing a Premier Domestic Solar and BESS Platform2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:42ZLexisNexisFH Capital to Acquire 75.1% Majority Stake in JinkoSolar's U.S. Manufacturing Operations, Establishing a Premier Domestic Solar and BESS PlatformNEW YORK

FH Capital , a leading private equity firm focused on clean-energy investments, today announced it has entered into a definitive agreement, through its designated affiliate, to acquire a 75.1% stake in JinkoSolar's (NYSE: JKS) U.S. subsidiary Jinko Solar (U.S.) Industries Inc.JinkoSolar will retain a 24.9% minority interest.

The transaction marks the transition to new controlling ownership of a state-of-the-art 2 gigawatts (GW) solar module manufacturing facility and a growing Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) business. Post-closing, FH Capital will deploy additional expansion capital to at least double the current solar module production capacity and initiate domestic BESS manufacturing. The new structure establishes a diverse U.S. manufacturing platform positioned to meet surging demand for high-performance solar and storage solutions, while aligning with the evolving U.S. clean-energy policy landscape.

FH Capital is led by Managing Partner Sanjeev Chaurasia, a veteran renewable energy investment banker and investor with more than 23 years of sector experience. Mr. Chaurasia previously served as Managing Director at Credit Suisse, where he co-founded the firm's renewable energy practice, headed global coverage of solar investment banking and led JinkoSolar's IPO on the NYSE in 2010.

"The transaction leverages JinkoSolar's well established seven-year U.S. manufacturing presence, proven technology, and strong relationships with blue-chip U.S. customers, with FH Capital's capabilities to create and manage a dynamic platform positioned to meet rising demand for domestic solar and storage solutions," said Chaurasia.

"FH Capital brings deep sector expertise, financing experience, and a deep understanding of the U.S. market," said Nigel Cockroft, U.S. General Manager of JinkoSolar. "We believe this transaction provides the right ownership, management and strategic direction for this new venture to grow capacity and serve the growing demand for high performance U.S.-sourced renewable energy products."

The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals. Financial terms were not disclosed.

Latham & Watkins LLP is serving as legal counsel to FH Capital. Morgan Stanley Asia Limited is serving as financial advisor to JinkoSolar and Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton (Hong Kong) is serving as its legal counsel.

About FH Capital

FH Capital is a private equity firm focused on growth-oriented clean energy investments. FH Capital combines deep sector expertise with financing capabilities and long-standing industry relationships to deliver needed capital to growing companies in the sector.

About JinkoSolar Holding Co., Ltd.

JinkoSolar (NYSE: JKS) is a global leader in clean energy technology. JinkoSolar distributes its solar products and sells its solutions and services to a diversified international utility, commercial and residential customer base in China, the United States, Japan, Germany, the United Kingdom, Chile, South Africa, India, Mexico, Brazil, the United Arab Emirates, Italy, Spain, France, Belgium, Netherlands, Poland, Austria, Switzerland, Greece and other countries and regions.

JinkoSolar had over 10 productions facilities globally, over 20 overseas subsidiaries in Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, India, Turkey, Germany, Italy, Switzerland, the United States, Mexico, and other countries, and a global sales network with sales teams in China, the United States, Canada, Brazil, Chile, Mexico, Italy, Germany, Turkey, Spain, Japan, the United Arab Emirates, Netherlands, Vietnam and India, as of March 31, 2026.

To find out more, please see: www.jinkosolar.com

urn:contentItem:6JHR-KV33-S2T4-W28C-00000-00MILBANK LLP - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:43ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK AGAIN RECOGNIZED AMONG TOP TEN MOST PRESTIGIOUS LAW FIRMS IN 2027 VAULT RANKINGS

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK DISCUSSES SUPREME COURT AT DRI'S 2026 LIFE, HEALTH, DISABILITY AND ERISA SEMINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta.

Ms. Sinzdak provided an overview of recent Supreme Court decisions that are relevant to the field. A distinguished Supreme Court attorney, Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and has briefed scores more, including matters related to ERISA and healthcare law.

The conference is a key event for lawyers in the health, insurance, and ERISA industry. You can learn more here.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED ELLIOTT IN CONNECTION WITH ITS INVESTMENT IN STRATOLAUNCH

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP GOVERNANCE OF FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS & THIRD-PARTY RISK MANAGEMENT

On May 19, Willem Van de Wiele, National Partner in Willkie's Corporate & Financial Services Department, will chair the Governance of Financial Institutions & Third-Party Risk Management conference, hosted by CREOBIS in Brussels, Belgium.

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP BRIAN HAMILTON QUOTED IN LAW360 ON Q1 2026 GLOBAL M&A ACTIVITY

Brian Hamilton, a partner in the M&A and Private Equity Practice Groups, was quoted in a Law360 article examining key takeaways from PitchBook's latest global M&A report. The article reports that global M&A activity "surged to record highs" in the first quarter of 2026, reflecting "a market shaped by diverging trends across sectors, geographies, and buyer types."

Source: Company Website

Turnarounds & Workouts spotlighted Willkie's work as restructuring counsel to Saks Global Holdings LLC in an article on the progress of the luxury retail giant's closely watched chapter 11 case.

New York, NY (May 4, 2026) - Willkie Farr & Gallagher today announced that Jason Loden has joined the Firm as a partner in its Executive Compensation and Employee Benefits Department. He is based in Willkie's Dallas office.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [6 Press Releases]

+ LATHAM NAMED CAPITAL MARKETS FIRM OF THE YEAR

Latham & Watkins was named Capital Markets Firm of the Year at the Legal 500 Southeast Asia Awards 2026. The annual awards program recognizes the law firms and lawyers whose expertise continues to drive the region's most prominent industries and corporations.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES SIXTH STREET ON GROWTH INVESTMENT FOR THE USS LARGEST HOME REMODELING COMPANY, POWER HOME REMODELING

Power Home Remodeling (POWER), the nation's largest home remodeling company, has announced that it has secured a growth investment from Bain Capital, Sixth Street, and Harvest Partners Structured Capital. Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM ADVISES ON NUMEROUS ENERGY & INFRASTRUCTURE DEALS HONORED BY IJGLOBAL AND PROXIMO

Latham &Watkins continues to stand as the leader in energy and infrastructure (E&I), advising on a number of deals that were recognized in the 2025 IJGlobal Awards and Proximo's 2025 Deals of the Year report.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP: LITIGATOR OF THE WEEK RUNNERS-UP AND SHOUT-OUTS

Shout-out to Latham & Watkins partners Josh Hamilton, Manny Abascal and Chris Harris, counsel Lauren Barnett and associate Chandler Howell. The Latham team has represented City National Bank in litigation stemming from the $700 million Ponzi scheme run by former actor Zachary Horwitz through his company 1inMM Capital-both former City National customers. This week, a special referee appointed to oversee claims the 1inMM receiver brought against City National denied a request to reconsider an earlier decision dismissing all claims against the bank.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES ON SEAPORT THERAPEUTICS UPSIZED US$254.9 MILLION INITIAL PUBLIC OFFERING

Seaport Therapeutics, Inc., (Nasdaq: SPTX), a clinical-stage therapeutics company that is inventing and developing neuropsychiatric medicines, has announced the pricing of its upsized initial public offering of 14,160,000 shares of its common stock at a price to the public of US$18.00 per share, at the top of the target range.

Source: Company Website

+ LATHAM & WATKINS ADVISES CRAZY MOUNTAIN IN US$15 MILLION SERIES SEED FINANCING

Crazy Mountain, a new non‑alcoholic beer brand co‑founded by George Clooney, Rande Gerber, and Mike Meldman, has announced a US$15 million Series Seed financing led by CAVU Consumer Partners, with participation from Coatue and Discovery Land Company, which together incubated the business alongside the founding team.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [4 Press Releases]

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: LIFE SCIENCES ANTITRUST: RECENT EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENFORCEMENT AND TRENDS

The European Commission appears to be advancing a more expansive antitrust agenda in life sciences - one increasingly focused on innovation, exclusionary conduct and strategic interference with future competition. Counsel Michael Frese and European counsel Georgios Zacharodimos discuss how recent cases highlight the EC's efforts to prevent innovation suppression, unfair practices and anticompetitive conduct across the pharmaceutical sector, where merger reviews, abuse investigations and cartel enforcement are reshaping industry dynamics.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: BANK OF ENGLAND REVISES ITS PROPOSED REGIME FOR REGULATING SYSTEMICSTABLECOIN

Bank of England proposals for regulating sterling-denominated stablecoins have shifted since 2023, but remain significantly more onerous than the FCA's proposals for non-systemic stablecoins in many respects. Partners Simon Toms and Sebastian Barling and associate Joseph Kamyar discuss the bank's latest proposals - including criteria for systemic importance, asset composition, capital and reserve standards, holding limits, legal claims and safeguarding measures - as well as the next steps for consultation and implementation.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NINTH CIRCUIT ENJOINS CALIFORNIA CORPORATE CLIMATE RISK DISCLOSURE LAW, BUT THE WAITING GAME CONTINUES

On November 18, 2025, the Ninth Circuit stayed the enforcement of a California law requiring certain companies to publish a climate risk report. Whether companies will need to disclose their greenhouse gas emissions in 2026 remains an open question. Partners Raquel Fox and Virginia Milstead, counsel Caroline Kim and Liz Malone and associate Sydney Smith discuss the legal challenges, ongoing regulatory uncertainties and implications for companies' compliance planning.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NEW YEAR, NEW POLITICAL LAW TRENDS

Spending on federal lobbying reached an all-time high of approximately $4.5 billion in 2024 and continues to increase. Federal political action committees (PACs) raised $25.3 billion in the 2020 election cycle and $25.4 billion in the 2024 cycle, both more than doubling any previous election cycle, according to Federal Election Commission data. This upward trend continues in the 2026 cycle.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 Press Releases]

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT MARK SKERRY REFERENCED IN MLEX ON CFIUS PRIORITIES AND OUTLOOK FOR 2026

Partner Mark Skerry was referenced in an MLex article examining the priorities and policy direction of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) last year and the trends to look for in 2026. Mark provided insights on emerging regulatory measures regarding foreign investment, noting that while CFIUS will continue to address national security threats, other federal and state-level measures shouldn't be overlooked. These measures, Mark observed, will have a significant impact on investment into and out of the United States in the coming year.

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER REPRESENTS INITIAL PURCHASERS IN $350 MILLION SUBORDINATED NOTES OFFERING BY TXNM

Simpson Thacher recently represented the initial purchasers, led by MUFG, Citigroup and RBC Capital Markets, in connection with an offering of $350 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.000% Fixed-to-Fixed Reset Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056 by TXNM Energy, Inc. ("TXNM"). TXNM intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay short-term debt and for general corporate purposes.

TXNM is an energy holding company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Through its regulated utilities, Public Service Company of New Mexico and Texas-New Mexico Power Company, TXNM delivers energy to more than 800,000 homes and businesses across New Mexico and Texas.

The Simpson Thacher team for the transaction included Mark Brod, Barry Lau and Emily Anne Owen (Capital Markets); Jon Cantor and Jemma Li (Tax); and Jennie Getsin (FINRA and Blue Sky).

Source: Company Website

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT GEOFF BAILHACHE AND JAMES HOWE QUOTED IN LAW.COM ON M&A OUTLOOK IN EUROPE

Geoff Bailhache and James R. Howe, co-heads of the Firm's European M&A practice, were quoted in Law.com on what to expect from dealmaking activity in 2026. James explained how "European deal activity strengthened notably through the second half of 2025, with large cap and mid-market transactions rebounding as assets that had been held back earlier in the year finally came to market." Geoff also highlighted "despite ongoing macroeconomic challenges, an improving IPO market in both the U.S. and Europe has provided an important tailwind, reinforcing confidence and helping to stimulate M&A activity."

Source: Company Website

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP ON TAX WIEBE DIJKSTRA OF DE BRAUW BLACKSTONE WESTBROEK

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ DAVIS POLK RANKED AT TOP OF SYNDICATED FINANCE LEAGUE TABLES IN Q1 2026

Davis Polk once again captured top rankings for legal advisors across multiple categories in Q1 2026 syndicated lending league tables from LSEG LPC and Debtwire.

According to LSEG, Davis Polk advised on 79 syndicated loan financings totaling over $133 billion, ranking top five in 21 of 22 global and U.S. syndicated loans categories. In Debtwire's rankings, we are listed as advisor on four of the top 10 largest leveraged deals in Q1 2026, including the financing for the largest LBO ever to date: financing for the acquisition of Electronic Arts.

Source: Company Website

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK AGAIN RECOGNIZED AMONG TOP TEN MOST PRESTIGIOUS LAW FIRMS IN 2027 VAULT RANKINGS

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK DISCUSSES SUPREME COURT AT DRI'S 2026 LIFE, HEALTH, DISABILITY AND ERISA SEMINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta.

Ms. Sinzdak provided an overview of recent Supreme Court decisions that are relevant to the field. A distinguished Supreme Court attorney, Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and has briefed scores more, including matters related to ERISA and healthcare law.

The conference is a key event for lawyers in the health, insurance, and ERISA industry. You can learn more here.

Source: Company Website

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: LIFE SCIENCES ANTITRUST: RECENT EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENFORCEMENT AND TRENDS

The European Commission appears to be advancing a more expansive antitrust agenda in life sciences - one increasingly focused on innovation, exclusionary conduct and strategic interference with future competition. Counsel Michael Frese and European counsel Georgios Zacharodimos discuss how recent cases highlight the EC's efforts to prevent innovation suppression, unfair practices and anticompetitive conduct across the pharmaceutical sector, where merger reviews, abuse investigations and cartel enforcement are reshaping industry dynamics.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: BANK OF ENGLAND REVISES ITS PROPOSED REGIME FOR REGULATING SYSTEMICSTABLECOIN

Bank of England proposals for regulating sterling-denominated stablecoins have shifted since 2023, but remain significantly more onerous than the FCA's proposals for non-systemic stablecoins in many respects. Partners Simon Toms and Sebastian Barling and associate Joseph Kamyar discuss the bank's latest proposals - including criteria for systemic importance, asset composition, capital and reserve standards, holding limits, legal claims and safeguarding measures - as well as the next steps for consultation and implementation.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NINTH CIRCUIT ENJOINS CALIFORNIA CORPORATE CLIMATE RISK DISCLOSURE LAW, BUT THE WAITING GAME CONTINUES

On November 18, 2025, the Ninth Circuit stayed the enforcement of a California law requiring certain companies to publish a climate risk report. Whether companies will need to disclose their greenhouse gas emissions in 2026 remains an open question. Partners Raquel Fox and Virginia Milstead, counsel Caroline Kim and Liz Malone and associate Sydney Smith discuss the legal challenges, ongoing regulatory uncertainties and implications for companies' compliance planning.

Source: Company Website

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P.: NEW YEAR, NEW POLITICAL LAW TRENDS

Spending on federal lobbying reached an all-time high of approximately $4.5 billion in 2024 and continues to increase. Federal political action committees (PACs) raised $25.3 billion in the 2020 election cycle and $25.4 billion in the 2024 cycle, both more than doubling any previous election cycle, according to Federal Election Commission data. This upward trend continues in the 2026 cycle.

Source: Company Website

DavisPolk counsel Jarret Erickson has been named an "Outstanding Young Restructuring Lawyer" by Turnarounds & Workouts. The list recognizes the most accomplished corporate restructuring lawyers under the age of 40.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-GTK3-S197-Y2SR-00000-00KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:43ZLexisNexisSTOCKKOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION WELCOMES JAEHYUN JOO AS NEW CRO

Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) has appointed Jaehyun Joo as the new Chief Risk Officer (CRO) and Executive Vice President of its Investment Management Division. This strategic move was announced on April 17, 2026, following a thorough review by the Executive Committee.Jaehyun Joo will begin his three-year term on this day, bringing a wealth of experience from his previous roles. He joined Korea Ratings KR in 1999 and transitioned to the Bank of Korea in December 2005. Over the years, he has held several key positions, including Head of the Investment Strategy Team, Head of the Investment Planning Team, and most recently, the Head of the Reserve Management Group.In his new role at KIC, Joo aims to leverage his extensive background in international finance to enhance the corporation's investment management framework. He expressed a commitment to strengthening KIC's foundation for long-term and stable asset management, which is crucial for navigating the complexities of global markets.The appointment of Jaehyun Joo is seen as a pivotal step for KIC, particularly as the organization seeks to bolster its risk management capabilities amid evolving economic conditions. His expertise is expected to play a significant role in advancing KIC's strategic objectives and improving investment outcomes.As KIC continues to expand its global footprint, the insights and leadership of Jaehyun Joo will be instrumental in driving the corporation's mission forward, ensuring it remains a key player in the international investment landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-SVN3-S56J-J2HW-00000-00MILBANK LLP - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:43ZLexisNexisSTOCKMILBANK LLP - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: MILBANK LLP

PermID4297973195Websitehttps://www.milbank.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress55 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001-2163

ACTIVITIES:

Milbank LLP, founded in 1866, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 800 lawyers across 12 offices, it serves clients in finance, energy, and infrastructure, specializing in project finance, M&A, and litigation. Generating over $1 billion annually, Milbank advised on major 2024 energy deals, per its recognition by Columbia Law School. Its culture fosters collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work, with 247 attorneys registered in New York. Competing with Sullivan & Cromwell, Milbank stands out for its finance expertise, with attorneys like John D. Franchini noted for excellence. In 2024, it expanded its renewable energy and AI practices. Recent initiatives include digital transaction platforms and ESG-focused financing, aligning with market demands.

+ MILBANK LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MILBANK AGAIN RECOGNIZED AMONG TOP TEN MOST PRESTIGIOUS LAW FIRMS IN 2027 VAULT RANKINGS

Milbank LLP is ranked 9th for overall prestige in the 2027 Vault Law Rankings, which are based on an annual survey of more than 20,000 associates.

In addition to achieving the #9 overall ranking, Milbank is rated the #1 firm for Project Finance and is among the top 10 for Clean Tech/Renewable Energy (#2), Banking & Financial Services (#5), Bankruptcy/Restructuring (#6), Energy, Oil & Gas (#6), General Corporate Practice (#9), Securities/Capital Markets (#9), Mergers & Acquisitions (#10) and Private Equity (#10). The firm is also ranked in the Securities Litigation (#19), General Commercial Litigation (#21), Business Outlook (#30) and Formal Training (#29) categories.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANK PARTNER COLLEEN ROH SINZDAK DISCUSSES SUPREME COURT AT DRI'S 2026 LIFE, HEALTH, DISABILITY AND ERISA SEMINAR

Milbank LLP Litigation & Arbitration partner Colleen Roh Sinzdak spoke at the Defense Research Institute's (DRI) 2026 Life, Health, Disability and ERISA Seminar, held April 29 - May 1 in Atlanta.

Ms. Sinzdak provided an overview of recent Supreme Court decisions that are relevant to the field. A distinguished Supreme Court attorney, Ms. Sinzdak has argued 11 cases before the Court and has briefed scores more, including matters related to ERISA and healthcare law.

The conference is a key event for lawyers in the health, insurance, and ERISA industry. You can learn more here.

Source: Company Website

+ MILBANKS ANTITRUST TEAM PUBLISHES ARTICLE IN CPI ON DOJS RENEWED SCRUTINY OF ENERGY AND DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE MARKETS

Milbank LLP Antitrust partners Adam Di Vincenzo and Fiona Schaeffer and associate John Ceccio authored an article published by Competition Policy International (CPI) examining the recent signaling by the Department of Justice (DOJ) of heightened interest in electricity generation and data center transactions.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED KKR IN THE FORMATION OF ITS $2.5 BILLION ASIA PRIVATE CREDIT FUNDRAISE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised KKR in the formation of its $2.5 billion Asia private credit fundraise focused on committing capital to privately originated performing credit investments in Asia Pacific. For more information, please see the company's press release.

The Debevoise team was led by investment management partner Lorna Bowen and included partner Sheena Paul, counsel Christina Chen Marex and Huiyu Yin and associates Charlie Altman, Ali Curry, Chris Duff, Zach Ferguson, Ruth Fruchter, Emma Hauber, Patrick Zuliang Li, Lawson Miralha, Julia Glazer Needle, James Tai, Johanna Waber and Eike Bjorn Weidner, and tax partners Stephen Jordan and Daniel Priest and associates Shanna Adler and Jeannie Le.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE ADVISED ELLIOTT IN CONNECTION WITH ITS INVESTMENT IN STRATOLAUNCH

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised Elliott Investment Management in connection with its investment in Stratolaunch, a hypersonics technology accelerator, to support the expansion of the company's hypersonic production and flight capabilities. For more information, please see the press release.

Source: Company Website

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP: MORE THAN $21 BILLION OF CFO TRANSACTIONS CLOSED IN PAST NINE MONTHS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has advised its clients across multiple Collateralized Fund Obligation (CFO) transactions with an aggregate value exceeding $21 billion over the past nine months. In addition to these CFO transactions, the Debevoise investment management and finance teams have advised clients in many single fund rated feeder transactions over the same time period.

Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [3 Press Releases]

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FDIC RESCINDS SUPERVISORY GUIDANCE ON MULTIPLE RE-PRESENTMENT NON-SUFFICIENT FUNDS FEES

Financial institutions commonly charge NSF fees when checks or Automated Clearinghouse ("ACH") transactions are presented for payment but cannot be covered by the balance in a customer's transaction account. After a check or ACH payment has been declined, it may later be resubmitted for payment (e.g., by a merchant), which may result in the customer's financial institution charging an additional NSF fee for the same transaction if the customer's account does not then reflect the necessary balance. The FDIC refers to these additional NSF fees as re-presentment NSF fees.

In its now-rescinded guidance, the FDIC had stated that failing to "clearly and conspicuously" disclose a practice of charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees could be both "deceptive" and "unfair" and therefore potentially a violation of Section 5 of the Federal Trade Commission Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. The FDIC had also stated that consumer disclosures may not be sufficient to address potential unfairness to consumers, especially if "multiple NSF fees are assessed for the same transaction in a short period of time without sufficient notice or opportunity for customers to bring their account to a positive balance in order to avoid the assessment of additional NSF fees." The FDIC's guidance also described additional risks related to charging multiple re-presentment NSF fees, as well as risk mitigation practices and the FDIC's supervisory approach to addressing consumer harm. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: GENIUS ACT IMPLEMENTATION

On April 7, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (the "FDIC") issued a notice of proposed rulemaking (the "FDIC NPR")[1] to implement provisions of the "Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act" or the "GENIUS Act.[2] The FDIC NPR proposes requirements that would apply to FDIC-supervised permitted payment stablecoin issuers ("PPSIs"), which in all cases would be subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions ("IDIs"). The FDIC also proposes clarifications to its deposit insurance regulations, including that deposits held as reserves for a payment stablecoin would not be insured on a pass-through basis. The FDIC NPR also sets forth updates to the FDIC's regulations to provide that the technology or recordkeeping used by an IDI to record its deposit liabilities does not affect whether those liabilities constitute "deposits." This update would clarify that a tokenized deposit that meets the statutory definition of "deposit" under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (the "FDI Act") is a deposit and is treated no differently under the FDI Act than other forms of deposits. Source: Company Website

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL: FEDERAL RESERVE PROPOSES EXPANSION THAT WOULD PERMIT USE OF FEDNOW FOR CROSS-BORDER PAYMENTS

On April 8, 2026, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System issued a proposal to amend Subpart C of Regulation J, which governs the FedNow® Service. The proposal would permit FedNow participants to use intermediaries other than Reserve Banks to send funds transfers through FedNow.[1] This change would permit FedNow to be used for cross-border payments, aligning FedNow with the Fedwire® Funds Service, which is widely used for cross-border payments and has "permitted intermediaries for decades."

Currently, banks that are FedNow participants may not use intermediaries (other than Federal Reserve Banks) for funds transfers sent through FedNow.[2] This means that FedNow is not currently available for cross-border payments because both the originator and the beneficiary must be customers of U.S. banks. When the Federal Reserve announced details of FedNow in 2020, it acknowledged this limitation to domestic payments, but noted plans to consider expanding to cross-border payments in the future.[3]

In its current proposal, the Federal Reserve indicated that it expects that allowing participants to use intermediaries other than the Reserve Banks would enable cross-border payments to be processed through FedNow. In particular, either the sending U.S. bank or receiving U.S. bank would be permitted to act as a correspondent bank for a non-U.S. bank. As a result, an international portion of a payment could be processed through that correspondent bank outside of FedNow, with FedNow being used for the domestic portion of the payment.

Comments on the proposal will be due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register. Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP [6 Press Releases]

+ WILLKIE RECOGNIZED BY TURNAROUNDS & WORKOUTS FOR LEADING SAKS GLOBALS RESTRUCTURING

Turnarounds & Workouts spotlighted Willkie's work as restructuring counsel to Saks Global Holdings LLC in an article on the progress of the luxury retail giant's closely watched chapter 11 case.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE ADDS EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS PARTNER JASON LODEN IN DALLAS

New York, NY (May 4, 2026) - Willkie Farr & Gallagher today announced that Jason Loden has joined the Firm as a partner in its Executive Compensation and Employee Benefits Department. He is based in Willkie's Dallas office.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE ADVISES HEENEY CAPITAL ON OFFTAKE AGREEMENTS FOR VENEZUELAN BULK COMMODITIES AND GOLD PROJECTS

Willkie is representing Heeney Capital in connection with offtake arrangements and associated investment across Venezuelan bulk commodities and gold projects, in conjunction with Mercuria.

Heeney Capital, in conjunction with Mercuria, has secured offtake arrangements across Venezuelan bulk commodities and gold projects. These arrangements, supported by associated investment, are expected to unlock approximately US$2.2 billion in annual mineral export value, a tangible signal of U.S. commitment to improving economic conditions in and the development of Venezuela.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP: DOES IBM'S SETTLEMENT USHER IN A NEW ERA OF CORPORATE EXPOSURE UNDER THE FALSE CLAIMS ACT FOR DEI PROGRAMS?

Since the beginning of the second Trump administration, the United States Department of Justice (the "Department") has repeatedly expressed its intent to use the False Claims Act ("FCA") to target Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion ("DEI") practices at companies. Announced last May, the Department's Civil Rights Fraud Initiative (the "Initiative") was framed as a push to scrutinize and challenge DEI programs it determined were unlawfully discriminatory. On April 10, 2026, the Department announced a $17 million False Claims Act settlement with IBM-the first resolution under the Initiative.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE ADVISES CARIS LIFE SCIENCES ON NEW $1.2 BILLION CREDIT FACILITY

Willkie represented Caris Life Sciences (NASDAQ: CAI), a leader in molecular profiling for diagnosing and treating cancer, in connection with its $1.2 billion credit facility agented by Blue Owl Capital Corporation, providing for senior secured credit facilities consisting of $400 million in initial term loans, $300 million in Delayed Draw Term Loans and $500 million in uncommitted incremental term loans. Proceeds from the initial term loans were used to pay off Caris's existing third-party debt and for general corporate purposes.

Source: Company Website

+ WILLKIE FARR & GALLAGHER LLP: ALLYSON B. SMITH RECOGNIZED AS 2026 OUTSTANDING YOUNG RESTRUCTURING LAWYER BY TURNAROUNDS & WORKOUTS

Willkie partner Allyson B. Smith has been recognized by Turnarounds & Workouts as an "Outstanding Young Restructuring Lawyer" for 2026. She was selected as one of 12 attorneys under the age of 40 for their work on the past year's notable bankruptcies and restructurings.

Source: Company Website

Power Home Remodeling (POWER), the nation's largest home remodeling company, has announced that it has secured a growth investment from Bain Capital, Sixth Street, and Harvest Partners Structured Capital. Source: Company Website
