DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION WELCOMES JAEHYUN JOO AS NEW CRO

Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) has appointed Jaehyun Joo as the new Chief Risk Officer (CRO) and Executive Vice President of its Investment Management Division. This strategic move was announced on April 17, 2026, following a thorough review by the Executive Committee.Jaehyun Joo will begin his three-year term on this day, bringing a wealth of experience from his previous roles. He joined Korea Ratings KR in 1999 and transitioned to the Bank of Korea in December 2005. Over the years, he has held several key positions, including Head of the Investment Strategy Team, Head of the Investment Planning Team, and most recently, the Head of the Reserve Management Group.In his new role at KIC, Joo aims to leverage his extensive background in international finance to enhance the corporation's investment management framework. He expressed a commitment to strengthening KIC's foundation for long-term and stable asset management, which is crucial for navigating the complexities of global markets.The appointment of Jaehyun Joo is seen as a pivotal step for KIC, particularly as the organization seeks to bolster its risk management capabilities amid evolving economic conditions. His expertise is expected to play a significant role in advancing KIC's strategic objectives and improving investment outcomes.As KIC continues to expand its global footprint, the insights and leadership of Jaehyun Joo will be instrumental in driving the corporation's mission forward, ensuring it remains a key player in the international investment landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

value.Title: Hochul's climate goals are misguided and expensive
value.Overview: Press & Sun-Bulletin (Binghamton, New York), NEWS; Pg. B10, 629words

urn:contentItem:6JGR-R7J3-RTFC-W3S3-00000-00Hochul's climate goals are misguided and expensive2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:43ZLexisNexisNEWSPg. B10Hochul's climate goals are misguided and expensive

Gov. Hochul gaslights again regarding reasons she can't implement New York's climate law, the Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act, due to affordability and other concerns. In a op-ed published in the Albany Times-Union, she implies that concerned New Yorkers who are defending the CLCPA and pushing for New YOrk to get serious about implementing it, in order to save a habitable climate, are "clinging to one's ideological beliefs."

This is insulting to me and is especially so because it appeared on the day after Earth Day. It seems Hochul is clinging to ideological beliefs, listening to the profit over people and profit over planet motivations of the fossil fuel industry.

Recent wars have surged fossil fuel prices, and underscored that fossil fuels are real drivers of the affordability crisis, not the CLCPA. Also, this governor is pushing for 5 gigawatts of new nuclear power, which can be more expensive than fossil fuels to generate power.

Renewable energy, backed up by energy storage, now provides the lowest cost electricity generation. States and countries with more renewable energy than New York typically have lower electricity prices.

Valdi Weiderpass

Endicott

Could Tioga County forests be turned over to energy developers?

The New York Senate passed legislation to sell about 600,000 acres of forest, which is public land, to renewable energy developers. The bill is now in the Assembly Standing Committee on Environmental Conservation. There are four cosponsors of Assembly Bill 10483.

The Senate version of this bill eliminates county and public interaction on these contracts.

Sen. Rachel May of Syracuse, the sponsor of the Senate bill states, "Local people often organize to make sure (energy projects) are not on their prime farmland ... so there is a push to put these kinds of facilities in places that are out of the way. And that means figuring out a way to get transmission lines through ... often through state reforestation lands."

Tioga County has 10,006 acres being considered for sale.

This plan could lead to disruption of the intended purposes for reforestation land, namely watershed protection and timber production. Environmental effects of these actions are unknown.

State and local communities should be working together to find other sites, such as parking lots, brownfields, rooftops and highway medians for the development of solar farms and other forms of renewable energy.

Nathaniel Taylor

Spencer

Trump continues war against environment, wildlife

Trump announced plans in April to decimate our country's forest resources. According to the Sierra Club, Trump finalized rules to fast-track approval of logging, mining, drilling, road building and other projects in America's national forests by eliminating public participation requirements for environmental reviews.

He also canceled public notices and comments on federal bird flu responses and wildlife-killing activities. Environmental reviews required under the National Environmental Policy Act are often the only way people can get information and provide input on the thousands of projects proposed each year on public lands.

Our forest lands are one of the country's greatest resources and are critical to environmental health. Claiming these natural resources are expendable, this Republican Administration has long sought to log, mine and pillage national lands. These actions will further worsen the environment and will put people at risk, threatening air and water quality, increasing harmful chemical exposure, and worsening global warming.

Trump has consistently shown preference to scale back environmental protections to pad corporate profits. He and his regime consistently put Americans last when it comes to protecting our health and wellbeing.

Rosanne Van Wie

Waverly

DASHBOARD: KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION

PermID5000352603Websitehttps://www.kic.kr/IndustrySovereign Wealth Fund

ACTIVITIES:

"The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC), established in 2005, is South Korea's sovereign wealth fund, tasked with managing national assets to enhance long-term wealth and support economic stability. Headquartered in Seoul, KIC manages approximately USD 207 billion in assets as of 2024, derived primarily from foreign exchange reserves and government contributions. KIC operates under the Korea Investment Corporation Act, reporting to the Ministry of Economy and Finance, with strategic oversight from a Steering Committee chaired by the Minister.

KIC's diversified portfolio spans equities (50.9%), fixed income (30.1%), alternative investments (18.1%), and cash (0.9%), focusing on global markets to maximize risk-adjusted returns. Key investments include public and private equities, real estate, infrastructure, and hedge funds, with a growing emphasis on sustainable sectors like renewable energy and technology. In 2023, KIC achieved a 5.2% return on its investments, driven by strong equity market performance and strategic allocations in AI and green infrastructure.

Led by CEO Jin Seoungho, KIC employs over 200 professionals and maintains offices in New York, London, and Singapore to facilitate global investments. The fund adheres to the Santiago Principles, ensuring transparency and sound governance. KIC's recent initiatives include a USD 500 million commitment to ESG-focused funds and partnerships with global asset managers to bolster South Korea's financial hub status. By balancing innovation and prudence, KIC strengthens South Korea's economic resilience."

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KOREA INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT'S NEW ETF CAPTIVATES INVESTORS WITH 100 BILLION WON MILESTONE

The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF, managed by Korea Investment Management Co., Ltd., has captivated the market by surpassing 100 billion won in net assets in less than a week since its listing. As of 22nd April 2026, the net asset value of the ETF, which debuted on the Korea Exchange on 14th April 2026, reached 103.7 billion won, representing a staggering ninefold increase from its initial 11 billion won on the first trading day.Unlike traditional ETFs that focus solely on aerospace and defense sectors, Korea Investment Management's active ETF is strategically concentrated on 'NewSpace' services. This includes companies engaged in reusable launch vehicles, artificial intelligence-based satellite data analytics, and space data centers. Notable holdings include EchoStar (22.65%), Rocket Lab, Planet Labs, Intuitive Machines, and MDA Space. EchoStar, which has a stake in SpaceX, holds the highest weighting among all ETFs listed in Korea, while MDA Space and York Space Systems are exclusive to this ETF.The ACE US Space Tech Active ETF has demonstrated impressive performance since its launch, delivering a return of 7.63%, the highest among the nine space and aerospace ETFs currently available in Korea. This strong performance has attracted significant interest from individual investors, who have cumulatively net purchased 52.6 billion won over the six trading days following the ETF's introduction.Kim Hyuntae, Head of Global Quantitative Investment at Korea Investment Management and the fund's portfolio manager, emphasized the importance of an agile approach to managing the ETF. He stated, 'Given that the space industry is still in the early stages of growth, we determined that it is important to construct a portfolio focused on high-growth, pure space technology companies and to respond quickly to market changes, so we launched the product as an active ETF.' He further noted that the active management allows for swift incorporation of new listings, such as SpaceX, directly responding to market events.As the space industry continues to evolve, Korea Investment Management's ACE US Space Tech Active ETF is positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities, appealing to investors seeking exposure to high-growth sectors. The rapid accumulation of assets within a short timeframe underscores the growing investor confidence in this innovative approach to space technology investments.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION WELCOMES JAEHYUN JOO AS NEW CRO

Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) has appointed Jaehyun Joo as the new Chief Risk Officer (CRO) and Executive Vice President of its Investment Management Division. This strategic move was announced on April 17, 2026, following a thorough review by the Executive Committee.Jaehyun Joo will begin his three-year term on this day, bringing a wealth of experience from his previous roles. He joined Korea Ratings KR in 1999 and transitioned to the Bank of Korea in December 2005. Over the years, he has held several key positions, including Head of the Investment Strategy Team, Head of the Investment Planning Team, and most recently, the Head of the Reserve Management Group.In his new role at KIC, Joo aims to leverage his extensive background in international finance to enhance the corporation's investment management framework. He expressed a commitment to strengthening KIC's foundation for long-term and stable asset management, which is crucial for navigating the complexities of global markets.The appointment of Jaehyun Joo is seen as a pivotal step for KIC, particularly as the organization seeks to bolster its risk management capabilities amid evolving economic conditions. His expertise is expected to play a significant role in advancing KIC's strategic objectives and improving investment outcomes.As KIC continues to expand its global footprint, the insights and leadership of Jaehyun Joo will be instrumental in driving the corporation's mission forward, ensuring it remains a key player in the international investment landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION LAUNCHES INTERNATIONAL FINANCE ACADEMY FOR 2026

The Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) announced its plan to host the 2026 First Half International Finance Academy, commencing on 20th April 2026. This initiative aims to enhance the expertise of finance professionals in both public and private sectors.The International Finance Academy will feature a series of eight lectures over four days, beginning with a comprehensive outlook on the U.S. stock market for the year 2026. Participants will also engage in discussions surrounding alternative investments, including infrastructure and private debt, along with post-investment portfolio management strategies from both investment management and operational perspectives.Key issues currently affecting financial markets will be addressed, such as the evolving U.S. interest rate environment and the implications stemming from the institutionalization of stablecoins. The program has garnered significant interest, receiving applications from 65 financial and investment institutions eager to participate.Park Ilyoung, CEO of KIC, emphasized the corporation’s commitment to the growth of the domestic financial industry, stating, 'The corporation has contributed to the development of the domestic financial industry by generously sharing its accumulated overseas investment experience and global network.' He further noted that KIC will continue to focus on nurturing global investment professionals through initiatives like the International Finance Academy.Since its inception in 2022, the International Finance Academy has aimed to bolster the capabilities of domestic financial investment professionals, reflecting KIC’s ongoing dedication to fostering a knowledgeable and skilled workforce in the financial sector.

+ KOREA INVESTMENT CORPORATION WELCOMES JAEHYUN JOO AS NEW CRO

Korea Investment Corporation (KIC) has appointed Jaehyun Joo as the new Chief Risk Officer (CRO) and Executive Vice President of its Investment Management Division. This strategic move was announced on April 17, 2026, following a thorough review by the Executive Committee.Jaehyun Joo will begin his three-year term on this day, bringing a wealth of experience from his previous roles. He joined Korea Ratings KR in 1999 and transitioned to the Bank of Korea in December 2005. Over the years, he has held several key positions, including Head of the Investment Strategy Team, Head of the Investment Planning Team, and most recently, the Head of the Reserve Management Group.In his new role at KIC, Joo aims to leverage his extensive background in international finance to enhance the corporation's investment management framework. He expressed a commitment to strengthening KIC's foundation for long-term and stable asset management, which is crucial for navigating the complexities of global markets.The appointment of Jaehyun Joo is seen as a pivotal step for KIC, particularly as the organization seeks to bolster its risk management capabilities amid evolving economic conditions. His expertise is expected to play a significant role in advancing KIC's strategic objectives and improving investment outcomes.As KIC continues to expand its global footprint, the insights and leadership of Jaehyun Joo will be instrumental in driving the corporation's mission forward, ensuring it remains a key player in the international investment landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ GOLDMAN SACHS AND ARDIAN ACQUIRE $1BN CIC PORTFOLIO AT NOTABLE DISCOUNTS

Goldman Sachs and Ardian have successfully acquired a $1 billion portfolio from China Investment Corporation (CIC) at significant discounts, underscoring their competitive positioning in the secondary market. On 15th April 2026, sources indicated that Ardian secured the assets at a double-digit discount, while Goldman achieved a single-digit discount, revealing the varied pricing across the underlying fund stakes involved in the transaction.CIC, which has previously built a substantial foothold in alternative assets, is currently rebalancing its portfolio to reduce its exposure to illiquid US dollar-denominated holdings. As of 2024, approximately 48.5% of CIC's assets were allocated to alternatives, with 62.5% of its global portfolio managed externally. This strategic shift comes as part of a broader initiative by CIC to streamline its investments and mitigate risks associated with market concentration.Initially, CIC explored the option of offloading stakes in funds managed by prominent private equity firms such as Carlyle, KKR, and TPG. However, after pausing the process, discussions were reopened, ultimately leading to the successful sale to Goldman and Ardian. This move highlights CIC's adaptability in navigating the complex landscape of asset management while responding to evolving market conditions.For Goldman and Ardian, this acquisition not only enhances their scale within the secondary market but also reinforces their long-standing strategies. Ardian, recognized as one of the most active buyers globally, has recently raised a record $30 billion secondary fund. In parallel, Goldman Sachs Asset Management has executed over 750 secondary transactions, deploying more than $80 billion in capital, further solidifying its expertise in this domain.The transaction also reflects broader market dynamics, with institutional investors—including notable entities like Harvard University and several Asian asset managers—turning increasingly to the secondaries market. This trend is driven by a need for liquidity and portfolio rebalancing in an environment marked by concerns over prolonged illiquidity and concentration risk. As such, the sale of CIC's portfolio represents a significant moment in the ongoing evolution of investment strategies among major institutional players.

+ BLUEFIVE CAPITAL TAKES CHARGE AS GP FOR CICC–HEBEI DEVELOPMENT FUND

BlueFive Capital, a global investment platform, has been appointed as the first General Partner (GP) for a Fund of Funds established by CICC Capital, the private equity arm of China International Capital Corporation (CICC), in partnership with Chinese steelmaker HBIS Group. This strategic move was officially marked by a ceremony held at HBIS Group's R&D centre in Beijing, China, on 6th January 2026.The Fund-of-Funds is set to deploy capital through a combination of fund investments and direct investments that align with HBIS Group's industrial priorities. These priorities encompass both traditional sectors and emerging industries, including advanced materials, new energy, and next-generation information technology. The Fund aims to raise RMB32 billion (approximately $4.58 billion) over a 15-year term, focusing on investments that foster cross-border opportunities and industrial upgrading.Structured around three key pillars—industry-finance synergy, central-local cooperation, and cross-border linkage—the fund aims to build a robust connection between local governments, international capital, and industrial advancement. BlueFive Capital will collaborate with leading industrial and financial institutions, as well as local partners, to ensure the long-term operational success of the fund.Hazem Ben-Gacem, Founder and Chief Executive of BlueFive Capital, expressed optimism regarding China's long-term growth potential. He stated, 'We are bullish about China’s long-term growth potential and see significant opportunities for HBIS Group’s cross-border expansion, particularly in high-end manufacturing and new building materials.' This sentiment reflects the firm’s strategy to identify high-quality investment opportunities both domestically and internationally.HBIS Group, recognized as one of the world’s largest steel materials manufacturers, has been featured in the Fortune Global 500 for 17 consecutive years. The company operates across four main business segments: steel materials, emerging industries, overseas businesses, and financial services, while also controlling multiple listed companies. Meanwhile, CICC, a prominent investment bank headquartered in Beijing, is known for its comprehensive financial services platform that spans investment banking, asset management, and private equity, among others. This partnership with BlueFive Capital is expected to enhance investment opportunities and bolster industrial growth in the region.

+ CHINA INVESTMENT CORP FUELS $60 BILLION CAPITAL SURGE IN CANADA

Foreign direct investment from China and Hong Kong in Canada exceeded $60 billion by the end of 2023, making these regions the third-largest source of overseas capital in the country. This significant figure marks a critical milestone in Canada’s economic landscape, with Alberta receiving approximately half of this investment. As reported by Statistics Canada, the investment stock from China stood at $24.9 billion, while Hong Kong contributed $32.8 billion, trailing only the United States and Europe in terms of foreign capital sources.On the 30th of December 2025, The Globe and Mail highlighted that Alberta's engagement with Chinese and Hong Kong investors has remained robust, even amidst challenging federal relations with Beijing. Rick Christiaanse, the chief executive of Invest Alberta since October 2021, noted a recent uptick in consultations between federal officials and the provincial agency, signaling a potential thaw in relations. Christiaanse referred to the current environment as 'harvest time' for those who persevered through previous political turbulence.Invest Alberta has reported that Chinese and Hong Kong investments are primarily focused on Alberta's traditional oil and gas sector, as well as carbon capture projects, green energy initiatives, and tourism development. Notably, China serves as a major customer for the expanding Trans-Mountain pipeline, underscoring the economic interdependence between the regions. This investment trend persists despite the backdrop of strained relations between Ottawa and Beijing, which deteriorated sharply during the Meng Wanzhou extradition case and the detention of two Canadian citizens from 2018 to 2021.The federal government has sought to reset ties with China, with notable interactions such as then-Foreign Minister M lanie Joly's visit to Beijing in July 2024. However, the imposition of tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles led to retaliatory measures against Canadian canola. The relationship has shown signs of improvement under Prime Minister Mark Carney, who met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October 2025. Global Affairs officials are reportedly preparing for a potential Carney visit to China in 2026, indicating a focus on diplomatic engagement.Christiaanse emphasized that Invest Alberta faced less domestic criticism than federal officials when engaging with Chinese counterparts, particularly prior to the current administration's shift toward foreign-policy realism. He asserted that the rationale for disengaging from China 'simply does not exist,' a sentiment reflecting the complex dynamics of international relations impacting foreign investment strategies. Overall, Canada remains primarily reliant on the United States for foreign direct investment, which constituted 45.7% of the total in 2023, while Asia-Oceania, led by Japan, Hong Kong, and China, accounted for 11.8%.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-3189-00000-00DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:43ZLexisNexisSTOCKDAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY TECHNOLOGY RAISES HK$4.4 BILLION IN HONG KONG LISTING BACKED BY MAJOR LAW FIRMS

Sigenergy Technology, a Shanghai-based maker of energy storage systems, has closed its Hong Kong initial public offering, raising HK$4.4 billion, roughly $562 million. The deal was steered by Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, which provided critical legal guidance throughout the listing and after-market processes. The listing was recorded on the 17th of April 2026. Launched in May 2022, Sigenergy focuses on distributed energy storage systems and provides stackable, solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions. The company commands a 28.6 percent share in the global market for stackable all-in-one DESS products, illustrating its position in a sector expected to expand as demand for clean energy storage grows. The IPO was underwritten by a consortium led by CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with China International Capital Corporation acting as joint sponsor. Underwriters were supported by Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ Christine Xu overseeing Greater China ECM and capital markets partner Lipton Li, alongside counsel Queenie Tong. In a broader deal flow for the region, Mindspace Business Parks REIT agreed to acquire the International Tech Park in Chennai for 30 billion rupees ($321 million), advised by Khaitan & Co. Separately, Indian asset manager SBI Funds Management is preparing for a 130-billion-rupee IPO, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising. The Sigenergy IPO underscores growing investor appetite for renewable energy and storage technologies.

+ DAVIS POLK-FOUNDED LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL CLOSES SIXTH FUND AT $2.5B

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has secured $2.5 billion for its sixth fund, signaling continued investor appetite for the manager's approach. The announcement was made public on April 14, 2026.The capital will be deployed in the financial services and technology sectors, areas the firm believes offer resilience and upside in the current market.Lightyear plans to back both established companies and innovative startups, leveraging its network and operating capabilities to help portfolio companies scale.Davis Polk & Wardwell has played a hands-on role, with its attorneys aiding in fund structuring and regulatory navigation, strengthening Lightyear's execution capabilities.Looking ahead, investors will be watching how Lightyear deploys the capital and whether it sustains strong returns amid macro and sector-specific challenges.

+ DAVIS POLK BETS ON LOS ANGELES GROWTH WITH NEW LA OFFICE

Davis Polk & Wardwell announced on 14th April 2026 that it plans to open a Los Angeles office and appoint Jason Russell to lead the expansion.Russell, who previously led Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's Los Angeles office and served on its policy committee, brings a track record of handling high-profile matters for clients including the NBA, Christie’s and MGA Entertainment.The firm said the hire will help establish a sustained presence in California and is expected to attract additional talent as the new office is staffed.The Los Angeles project reflects Davis Polk's strategy to broaden its geographic footprint and compete with other major California firms for marquee matters.As the opening progresses, observers will watch how Russell leverages his relationships to grow the firm's practice in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LONDON GROWTH PUSHES DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON TO A NEW REVENUE MILESTONE

Debevoise & Plimpton disclosed a 40% revenue jump in its London office to $268 million, announced on 7th April 2026.The growth is underpinned by stronger demand across corporate law and dispute resolution as clients seek comprehensive legal solutions.The firm has been selective in high-value engagements and has adapted to market shifts.Industry observers say the performance signals momentum, with plans to invest in talent and broaden service lines.As the legal landscape evolves, competition remains intense, making sustained financial momentum a priority for the firm.

+ SECOND CIRCUIT AFFIRMS YPF VICTORY, DISMISSING MORE THAN $16 BILLION IN CLAIMS

The Second Circuit upheld the district court's finding that YPF had no contractual obligations to Petersen Energia Inversora S.A.U., Petersen Energia S.A.U., or Eton Park Capital Management L.P., together with its associated funds. The ruling is a clear victory for YPF and its defense team against the plaintiffs, who also included litigation funder Burford Capital.

+ DEBEVOISE APPOINTS SULLIVAN KC AND POPOVA TO HEAD EUROPEAN DISPUTES

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has named Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately, signaling a deeper push into Paris, Frankfurt and Luxembourg. The announcement was made on the 17th of March, 2026 and reflects the firm's strategy to strengthen international dispute resolution capabilities across mainland Europe.The appointment establishes regional leadership intended to drive Debevoise's dispute resolution practice in Europe alongside its other regional leaders in Latin America and Asia.Catherine Amirfar, Co-Chair of the firm's global International Dispute Resolution Group, said that Sullivan and Popova will build on Debevoise's long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe and will continue delivering top-tier advocacy and strategic counsel to clients.Sullivan KC is recognized as a leading figure in international arbitration and public international law, and in the new role he will focus on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations for a diverse client base that includes companies and private equity sponsors; he also has experience as an arbitrator in significant cases.Popova brings extensive experience in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on managing and litigating complex disputes; her dual civil and common-law qualifications, along with fluency in multiple languages, position her to navigate cross-border disputes, and she previously served on the ICCInternational Court of Arbitration.With more than four decades of experience, Debevoise’s International Dispute Resolution Group has delivered outcomes across 125+ countries and matters over $110 billion, underscoring the firm's commitment to achieving significant results for clients.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ COHEN RECOUNTS HALF-CENTURY OF BANKING REGULATION AND CRISIS WORK

H. Rodgin "Rodge" Cohen, the longtime senior chair of Sullivan & Cromwell, has been at the center of banking regulation for more than five decades. On 19th February 2026, during an event hosted by NYU Law’s Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF), Cohen spoke with candor about the arc of the modern financial system, with Jay Clayton, former SEC chairman, and Edward Rock, ICGF co-director, providing additional perspective.Cohen traced regulatory evolution from the postwar era to today, emphasizing how landmark measures reshaped access to banking. He and Clayton highlighted the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act as turning points that broadened inclusion and redefined compliance. Clayton noted the shifts since the 1970s, while Cohen underscored how these changes altered banks’ operations and risk profiles.A defining chapter in Cohen’s career came during the 2008 global financial crisis, when he represented several major banks as policymakers moved to stabilize the system. He recalled the critical hours before the government’s intervention after Lehman Brothers’ collapse, saying, "We were on the edge of a cliff," underscoring the urgency of decisive action and solidifying his reputation as a leading financial-services lawyer. urn:contentItem:6JG9-94B3-RTKH-03PV-00000-00Press & Sun readers sound off on state of the environment | Letters2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:43ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS & US NEWSPress & Sun readers sound off on state of the environment | LettersLetters to the Editor, Binghamton Press & Sun BulletinHochul's climate goals are misguided and expensive

Gov. Hochul gaslights again regarding reasons she can’t implement New York's climate law, the Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act, due to affordability and other concerns. In a op-ed published in the Albany Times-Union, she implies that concerned New Yorkers who are defending the CLCPA and pushing for New YOrk to get serious about implementing it, in order to save a habitable climate, are "clinging to one's ideological beliefs."

This is insulting to me and is especially so because it appeared on the day after Earth Day. It seems Hochul is clinging to ideological beliefs, listening to the profit over people and profit over planet motivations of the fossil fuel industry.

Recent wars have surged fossil fuel prices, and underscored that fossil fuels are real drivers of the affordability crisis, not the CLCPA. Also, this governor is pushing for 5 gigawatts of new nuclear power, which can be more expensive than fossil fuels to generate power.

Renewable energy, backed up by energy storage, now provides the lowest cost electricity generation. States and countries with more renewable energy than New York typically have lower electricity prices.

Valdi Weiderpass

Endicott

Could Tioga County forests be turned over to energy developers?

The New York Senate passed legislation to sell about 600,000 acres of forest, which is public land, to renewable energy developers. The bill is now in the Assembly Standing Committee on Environmental Conservation. There are four cosponsors of Assembly Bill 10483.

The Senate version of this bill eliminates county and public interaction on these contracts.

Sen. Rachel May of Syracuse, the sponsor of the Senate bill states, “Local people often organize to make sure (energy projects) are not on their prime farmland … so there is a push to put these kinds of facilities in places that are out of the way. And that means figuring out a way to get transmission lines through … often through state reforestation lands.”

Tioga County has 10,006 acres being considered for sale.

This plan could lead to disruption of the intended purposes for reforestation land, namely watershed protection and timber production. Environmental effects of these actions are unknown.

State and local communities should be working together to find other sites, such as parking lots, brownfields, rooftops and highway medians for the development of solar farms and other forms of renewable energy.

Nathaniel Taylor

Spencer

Trump continues war against environment, wildlife

Trump announced plans in April to decimate our country's forest resources. According to the Sierra Club, Trump finalized rules to fast-track approval of logging, mining, drilling, road building and other projects in America’s national forests by eliminating public participation requirements for environmental reviews.

He also canceled public notices and comments on federal bird flu responses and wildlife-killing activities. Environmental reviews required under the National Environmental Policy Act are often the only way people can get information and provide input on the thousands of projects proposed each year on public lands.

Our forest lands are one of the country’s greatest resources and are critical to environmental health. Claiming these natural resources are expendable, this Republican Administration has long sought to log, mine and pillage national lands. These actions will further worsen the environment and will put people at risk, threatening air and water quality, increasing harmful chemical exposure, and worsening global warming.

Trump has consistently shown preference to scale back environmental protections to pad corporate profits. He and his regime consistently put Americans last when it comes to protecting our health and wellbeing.

Rosanne Van Wie

Waverly

urn:contentItem:6JK3-R6T3-RSHC-73K0-00000-00Alcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) Reports $65 Million Investment to Expand Mosjøen Foundry in Norway2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:42ZLexisNexisAlcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) Reports $65 Million Investment to Expand Mosjøen Foundry in Norway

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 10:15 EDT

Alcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) is investing $65 million to expand its foundry production at the Mosjøen smelter in Norway, a move that aligns with the companys plan to deliver low carbon aluminum products to customers in key markets and to begin incorporating recycled content into the casting process for the first time since the 12th May 2026 announcement. The plan follows a continued effort to grow sustainable capabilities in Europe.

SECTION 1 ALCOA RECENT PRESS RELEASES

SECTION 2 ALCOA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 ALCOA RECENT PRESS RELEASES

May 11: Alcoa announces dividend

Alcoa today announced a quarterly dividend of 10.0c per share. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 19, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 19, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, June 05.

May 11: Alcoa announces dividend

Alcoa today announced a quarterly dividend of 10.0c per share. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 19, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 19, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, June 05.

March 02: Alcoa announces dividend

Alcoa today announced a quarterly dividend of 10.0c per share. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, March 10, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, March 10, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 26.

SECTION 2 ALCOA ACTIVITIES

Activities

Alcoa Corporation is a leading global producer of bauxite, alumina, and aluminum, headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company operates across the full aluminum value chain, encompassing bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting and casting, serving diverse industries such as aerospace, automotive, packaging, and construction. Alcoa's core activities emphasize sustainable production practices, including low-carbon aluminum manufacturing to meet environmental standards. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been its aluminum products for electric vehicles and renewable energy infrastructure, driven by rising demand for lightweight, eco-friendly materials that enhance energy efficiency. This strategic focus has positioned Alcoa to capitalize on global trends toward decarbonization and electrification, while maintaining operational excellence and innovation in its core processes. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.

VISHAY INTERTECHNOLOGY ACTIVITIES

Vishay Intertechnology Inc. is a global leader in the design, manufacture, and supply of passive electronic components and discrete semiconductors, serving industries such as automotive, industrial, telecommunications, and consumer electronics. Founded in 1962 and headquartered in the United States, the company produces essential products including resistors, capacitors, inductors, and optoelectronic devices that enable efficient power management and signal processing in modern applications. Vishay's fastest-growing segments include automotive electronics, driven by the surge in electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, as well as Internet of Things (IoT) solutions and renewable energy technologies. These areas have benefited from increasing demand for energy-efficient components, allowing Vishay to expand its market presence and innovate in high-reliability products. With a commitment to quality and technological advancement, Vishay continues to strengthen its position as a key supplier in the evolving electronics industry. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Electrical components & equipment company by market capitalisation.

VISHAY INTERTECHNOLOGY ACTIVITIES

Vishay Intertechnology Inc. is a global leader in the design, manufacture, and supply of passive electronic components and discrete semiconductors, serving industries such as automotive, industrial, telecommunications, and consumer electronics. Founded in 1962 and headquartered in the United States, the company produces essential products including resistors, capacitors, inductors, and optoelectronic devices that enable efficient power management and signal processing in modern applications. Vishay's fastest-growing segments include automotive electronics, driven by the surge in electric vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, as well as Internet of Things (IoT) solutions and renewable energy technologies. These areas have benefited from increasing demand for energy-efficient components, allowing Vishay to expand its market presence and innovate in high-reliability products. With a commitment to quality and technological advancement, Vishay continues to strengthen its position as a key supplier in the evolving electronics industry. It is the NYSE's 9th largest Electrical components & equipment company by market capitalisation.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

LOS ANGELES, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, CBRE Group Inc. has shown some weakness in recent days. CBRE Group Inc. (NYSE:CBRE), the NYSE's 3rd largest Real estate company by market cap, dropped 1.4% during its previous trading session to close at $144.06.

CBRE Group Inc. (NYSE:CBRE) is a global leader in commercial real estate services, offering comprehensive solutions in property management, investment sales, and tenant representation. With a notable footprint in the data center sector, CBRE's $2 billion data center investment underpins its strategic focus on digital infrastructure and sustainable growth. Leveraging its data center services, the company enhances its technology capabilities through strategic acquisitions and platform development. CBRE's robust leasing services, exemplified by its Midtown Manhattan office leasing strategy, and recent New York office lease achievements, fortify its competitive position in the commercial real estate ecosystem.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 328 words

Tue May 5, 2026: CBRE's Strategic Move: Turner & Townsend Project Management Combination Finalized

CBRE Group, Inc. (NYSE:CBRE) has successfully completed the combination of its project management business with Turner & Townsend, a majority-owned subsidiary, as of the 5th of May, 2026. This strategic merger aims to enhance CBRE's capabilities in project management, leveraging Turner & Townsend's extensive expertise in programme management and cost consultancy across various sectors.

Vincent Clancy, who has been at the helm of Turner & Townsend as Board Chair and Chief Executive Officer since 2008, joins the CBRE Board of Directors following this consolidation. Under his leadership, Turner & Townsend has seen substantial growth, with revenue soaring from approximately US$225 million in 2008 to more than US$1.9 billion in 2023. This integration aligns with CBRE's objective to create an unmatched project management offering that can capitalize on trends in infrastructure development and green energy transition.

Bob Sulentic, Chair and Chief Executive Officer of CBRE, emphasized the significance of this merger, stating, "Our unified project management business represents an offering that is unmatched for its scale and breadth of capabilities." Sulentic further noted that Clancy's insights from running a global business will be invaluable as CBRE navigates key growth sectors. This combination is expected to enhance CBRE's competitive edge in project management services.

CBRE now owns 70% of the combined Turner & Townsend and CBRE Project Management business. Since Turner & Townsend became a majority-owned subsidiary in November 2021, its revenue has experienced an impressive compound annual growth rate exceeding 20%. The integration will allow CBRE to report Project Management results as a standalone business segment, providing improved transparency for investors and stakeholders.

Turner & Townsend, a global professional services firm with over 22,000 employees operating in more than 60 countries, specializes in project management, cost consultancy, and programme management in various sectors including real estate, infrastructure, and energy. The completion of this merger solidifies CBRE's position as a leader in the commercial real estate services sector while creating a highly diversified, resilient business model poised for future growth.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 277 words

Wed Apr 29, 2026: CBRE Group Inc. Pursues Growth with New Securities Offering

CBRE Group, Inc. has filed a Form 8-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to announce significant updates regarding its recent securities offering. The filing, dated 27th April 2026, reveals the successful closing of a noteworthy notes offering, which aims to bolster the company's financial position and support future growth initiatives.

The Form 8-K outlines that CBRE Group, Inc. has entered into an underwriting agreement with major financial institutions, including Wells Fargo Securities, BofA Securities, Citigroup Global Markets, and Scotia Capital (USA), to offer a new series of debt securities. According to the filing, the registration statement for these notes has been declared effective, allowing the company to proceed without any SEC-imposed stop orders or suspensions.

Proceeds from the offering are intended for general corporate purposes, which may include debt repayment or reinvestment in business growth. The filing reassures investors that there has been no material adverse change in CBRE's financial condition since the last reporting period, underscoring the company's solid governance and robust internal controls.

CBRE Group, Inc. emphasizes that forward-looking statements in the report are subject to various risks and uncertainties. The absence of any significant legal or regulatory actions against the company further bolsters investor confidence. Investors are encouraged to review the detailed prospectus regarding the notes for comprehensive information on interest rates, maturity, and other terms.

The completion of this substantial notes offering, coupled with the company's commitment to transparency and compliance, is likely to enhance investor sentiment toward CBRE Group, Inc. As the company continues to navigate the financial landscape, its strategic use of proceeds from this offering may play a crucial role in shaping future earnings and operational success.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 243 words

Tue Apr 28, 2026: CBRE Group Secures $750M in Senior Notes Amid Strong Market Demand

CBRE Group Inc. has successfully priced an offering of $750 million aggregate principal amount of 5.250% senior notes due 2036. The announcement was made on 28th April 2026, highlighting the company's ongoing efforts to optimize its capital structure and strengthen its financial position in a competitive real estate market.

The senior notes are being issued at a price equal to 98.947% of their face value, reflecting a robust interest rate of 5.250% per annum. The issuance will be executed by CBRE Services, a wholly owned subsidiary of CBRE Group, and will be fully guaranteed by the parent company, ensuring investor confidence and financial security.

The company anticipates that the net proceeds from the notes will amount to approximately $737 million. CBRE Group plans to utilize these funds primarily to repay borrowings under its existing commercial paper program, thereby enhancing its liquidity and reducing short-term debt obligations.

This strategic move comes as CBRE Group continues to navigate evolving market conditions and aims to bolster its core business segments. With a projected core EPS of $7.60 to $7.80 for fiscal year 2026, driven by a significant expected growth in critical infrastructure services, the company is positioning itself for long-term success.

The notes are expected to settle on May 4th, 2026, marking a significant step in CBRE Group's financial strategy. Investors and analysts alike will be closely monitoring the company's performance in the coming quarters as it implements its growth initiatives and manages its debt levels.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE

6.1 Activities

CBRE Group Inc., a leading global commercial real estate services and investment firm listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in a comprehensive range of activities including property leasing, sales, valuation, asset management, and facilities outsourcing. The company operates in over 100 countries, serving clients across sectors such as office, retail, industrial, and multifamily properties, while providing strategic advisory and transaction services to facilitate real estate decisions. Among its fastest-growing segments are global investment management, which has expanded through diversified fund offerings and acquisitions, and technology-driven solutions like data analytics and proptech, enhancing operational efficiency and client experiences. Additionally, occupier services, focused on workspace optimization and sustainability consulting, have surged due to rising demand for flexible and eco-friendly real estate strategies, positioning CBRE as a key player in adapting to evolving market dynamics. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Real estate company by market capitalisation.

CBRE Group Inc. (NYSE:CBRE) has announced a significant development in its Greater Boston operations, marking a pivotal moment in CBRE Greater Boston team expansion. The recent CBRE news Greater Boston highlights the appointment of new CBRE Vice Presidents Greater Boston, Shay Kenney CBRE and Brooke Howard CBRE, who join as CBRE Boston leadership. According to the CBRE press release Shay Kenney and CBRE press release Brooke Howard, these strategic hires will bolster the CBRE Greater Boston commercial real estate services. As CBRE appoints Shay Kenney and CBRE appoints Brooke Howard, both are expected to enhance CBRE Boston advisory services and CBRE Boston tenant representation. This CBRE leadership announcement showcases CBRE's commitment to expanding CBRE workplace solutions Boston and CBRE project management Boston. With a focus on CBRE Boston office leasing, CBRE Boston property management, and CBRE Boston capital markets, the company continues to advance in CBRE commercial brokerage Greater Boston to address CBRE recruitment Boston and CBRE career opportunities Boston dynamics.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

LOS ANGELES, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, CBRE Group Inc. has shown some weakness in recent days. CBRE Group Inc. (NYSE:CBRE), the NYSE's 3rd largest Real estate company by market cap, dropped 1.4% during its previous trading session to close at $144.06.

CBRE Group Inc. (NYSE:CBRE) is a global leader in commercial real estate services, offering comprehensive solutions in property management, investment sales, and tenant representation. With a notable footprint in the data center sector, CBRE's $2 billion data center investment underpins its strategic focus on digital infrastructure and sustainable growth. Leveraging its data center services, the company enhances its technology capabilities through strategic acquisitions and platform development. CBRE's robust leasing services, exemplified by its Midtown Manhattan office leasing strategy, and recent New York office lease achievements, fortify its competitive position in the commercial real estate ecosystem.
